unless: You can't enter the building unless you have a valid badge.
I won't go to the meeting unless they send the agenda in advance.
release: The band plans to release their new album in early spring next year.
The studio decided to release the film digitally before its theatrical debut.
dozens: There were dozens of people waiting outside the venue before the doors even opened.
She received dozens of messages after posting about her experience online.
in advance: You should book your train tickets in advance to get the best price available.
The teacher asked all students to prepare their questions in advance before the seminar.
swear word: Some audiences were surprised to hear a swear word in what was marketed as a family film.
Using a swear word in a formal presentation is generally considered unprofessional.
coffee grounds: She filtered out the coffee grounds by pouring the brew through a paper cone into the cup.
He always disposed of the coffee grounds in the garden compost instead of throwing them away.
poured: She poured the hot water slowly over the coffee grounds and waited for it to drip through.
He poured himself a glass of cold water and sat down to read the morning news.
reached: In the 1990s, country artists like Garth Brooks reached the top of both country and pop charts.
The campaign reached millions of people through social media in just a few days.
struck: A severe drought struck the region and forced thousands of families to abandon their farms.
Lightning struck the old oak tree at the edge of the field during the summer storm.
music labels: Several major music labels are headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee.
Independent artists often prefer to avoid large music labels and distribute their work directly.
unlike: Unlike espresso, filter coffee uses gravity rather than pressure to extract the flavour.
Unlike her sister, she preferred quiet evenings at home to loud social gatherings.
severe drought: A severe drought hit the region in the 1930s, turning farmland into vast fields of dust.
The government declared a state of emergency after a severe drought left thousands without water.
drip coffee: Filter coffee, also known as drip coffee, is made by letting hot water pass slowly through ground beans.
She preferred drip coffee over espresso because she liked a lighter, less intense flavour in the mornings.
stepped in: When the lead actor fell ill, his understudy stepped in and delivered a remarkable performance.
The government stepped in to prevent the company from collapsing and protect thousands of jobs.
crowd: A large crowd gathered in the square to watch the outdoor concert on a warm summer evening.
The café attracted the kind of crowd that enjoyed spending hours reading and talking over coffee.
brewed: She brewed a pot of coffee and set it on the table before her guests arrived.
The tea had been brewed for too long and tasted bitter by the time they finally sat down.
grown-ups: The children were told to wait outside while the grown-ups discussed the situation inside.
From a child's perspective, grown-ups always seem to know exactly what they are doing.
second thought: She accepted the invitation without a second thought, excited about the opportunity.
He signed the contract without giving it a second thought, which he later came to regret.
finely-ground: Turkish coffee requires very finely-ground beans to produce its characteristic thick and strong brew.
The recipe called for finely-ground black pepper rather than coarsely crushed.
selling out: Some fans accused the band of selling out when they signed with a major commercial label.
The venue kept selling out every night, which forced them to add extra shows to the tour.
step out: Could you step out of the car for a moment, please? I need to check your documents.

stitched together: The documentary was stitched together from dozens of interviews recorded over three years.
American English was stitched together from the dialects and languages of many different immigrant groups.
sludge: Turkish coffee leaves a thick layer of sludge at the bottom of the cup that you should not drink.
After the flood, the streets were covered in a layer of grey sludge that took days to clean up.
charts: The song debuted at number one on the charts and stayed there for six consecutive weeks.
Country music started reaching the pop charts in the 1980s, attracting a much wider audience.
a bit harsh: Calling someone's grammar mistake a sign of ignorance seems a bit harsh, don't you think?
The teacher's feedback was a bit harsh, but it pushed him to improve his writing significantly.
spread: The tradition of filter coffee spread across Europe and North America throughout the twentieth century.
News of the discovery spread quickly through the scientific community within just a few hours.
fiercely loyal: Country music fans are fiercely loyal and continue to support their favourite artists for decades.
She was fiercely loyal to her team, always defending them publicly even when they made mistakes.
bitter: Espresso can taste very bitter if the water temperature is too high or the extraction takes too long.
He still felt bitter about the way the project had been taken away from him without explanation.
to motor west: During the Dust Bowl, thousands of families packed their cars to motor west in search of a better life.

wider: Country music gradually appealed to a wider audience as it began blending with pop and rock sounds.
The new policy aims to give a wider range of students access to quality educational resources.
grab attention: The artist used bold colours and unusual shapes to grab attention in a crowded gallery.
A strong opening line is the best way to grab attention and keep the reader engaged from the start.
diverse: The city is home to a diverse mix of cultures, languages, and traditions from around the world.
A diverse team brings different perspectives that lead to more creative and effective solutions.
trace the origin: Researchers are trying to trace the origin of the idiom back to its earliest recorded use.
It is difficult to trace the origin of certain folk songs because they were passed down orally.
standardize: The government decided to standardize the spelling of the language across all official documents.
Companies often try to standardize their processes to improve consistency and reduce errors.
coherent: The essay was well-written and coherent, with each paragraph building logically on the previous one.
After all the revisions, the plan finally became coherent enough to present to the board.
thick dust: A layer of thick dust had settled on every surface in the abandoned house.
During the Dust Bowl, clouds of thick dust swept across the plains, blocking out the sun.
coincided with: The rise of bossa nova coincided with a period of great cultural optimism in Brazil.
The company's success coincided with a broader economic boom that lifted many businesses.
fully paved: Route 66 was fully paved by the early 1930s, making long road trips much more practical.

proved useful: The folded paper filter proved useful because it removed the gritty sediment from the coffee.
The extra preparation they had done proved useful when unexpected questions came up at the meeting.
wealth: The industrial revolution created immense wealth for factory owners while workers remained poor.
Nashville accumulated wealth and influence as the centre of the country music industry.
neon signs: The city's main street was lined with neon signs advertising cafés, hotels, and music venues.
She loved walking through the neighbourhood at night, when the neon signs lit up the puddles.
made up: American English is made up of elements from dozens of different languages and dialects.
The story he told turned out to be completely made up — there was no truth in it at all.
rooted in: Country music is rooted in the folk traditions and storytelling of rural America.
His philosophy was deeply rooted in the belief that education is a basic human right.
struggles: The lyrics of many blues songs describe the everyday struggles of working-class life.

southern: Country music has strong southern roots, drawing from folk, gospel, and blues traditions.

gambler: The song tells the story of an old gambler who gives advice to a young man on a train.

fellow: A fellow traveller sat down next to her and they started talking about their journeys.
He always respected his fellow musicians and gave credit to everyone who contributed to the record.
leave behind: Many families chose to leave behind everything they owned and head west in search of a better life.
She didn't want to leave behind the city she had grown up in, even though the job offer was tempting.
rural roots: Country music has always celebrated its rural roots, even as it expanded into mainstream pop.

cross over: Some country artists managed to cross over into pop music and reach a much broader audience.

well beyond: The popularity of country music spread well beyond the American South during the twentieth century.
The impact of the discovery went well beyond what the scientists had originally expected.
might never: Without the internet, independent artists might never have found an audience for their music.

went on to: He started out playing small venues and went on to sell out stadiums across the world.

hit the jackpot: The young singer hit the jackpot when her first single went viral and topped the charts overnight.

not otherwise: Social media gives artists access to audiences they might not otherwise be able to reach.
The grant allowed researchers to pursue work they would not otherwise have had funding for.
pleases: Creating content that pleases the algorithm does not always mean creating content that satisfies the audience.
A good designer creates work that both pleases the client and communicates the intended message clearly.
beverage: Coffee is the most widely consumed hot beverage in the world, enjoyed in dozens of different forms.
The café offered a wide selection of cold beverages for customers who preferred something refreshing.
sipped carefully: She sipped carefully from the tiny cup, aware that the coffee grounds were still settling at the bottom.
He sipped carefully at the hot tea, not wanting to burn his tongue after a long cold walk.
gritty taste: Early coffee drinkers often complained about the gritty taste left by loose grounds in the cup.
The cheaply processed cocoa left a gritty taste on the tongue that no amount of sugar could mask.
drips: In filter coffee, hot water drips slowly through the ground beans, extracting the flavour by gravity.
He watched the last of the coffee as it drips through the filter and fell into the waiting mug.
whether: He wasn't sure whether to use a paper filter or a metal one for the best flavour.

whip: To make Dalgona coffee, you whip instant coffee, sugar, and water together until the mixture becomes creamy.

foam: A thick layer of foam sits on top of the whipped coffee before it is stirred into the cold milk.

sits on top: The whipped coffee foam sits on top of the cold milk and slowly dissolves as you stir it in.

spelled: Dalgona coffee is spelled differently in various countries, reflecting how quickly the trend spread online.

address: The professor asked students to address him by his first name rather than a formal title.
In formal writing, it is important to address the reader directly and maintain a respectful tone.
caution you against: I would caution you against drinking Turkish coffee too quickly — the grounds need time to settle.

against: The barista cautioned him against stirring the Turkish coffee, explaining that the grounds would rise.

I dare you: I dare you to drink your Turkish coffee before the grounds have settled at the bottom.
She looked at him and said, 'I dare you to say that again in front of everyone.'
timekeeping: Before mechanical clocks, timekeeping was an imprecise practice that relied on natural cues like sunlight.
Accurate timekeeping became essential for railways to coordinate schedules across long distances.
hourglasses: Sailors once used hourglasses to measure time at sea when water clocks were not practical.
The museum displayed a collection of ornate hourglasses from the fifteenth and sixteenth centuries.
meant: Local time meant something different in every town before railways standardized the clocks.

profanity: Some style guides advise journalists to avoid profanity in published articles unless it is directly quoted.

criticized: The use of 'ain't' has been criticized by language prescriptivists for centuries, but it persists in everyday speech.
The policy was widely criticized by experts who argued it ignored the most vulnerable communities.
proof: The widespread survival of nonstandard forms in everyday speech is proof that language evolves naturally.
Scientists need concrete proof before they can publish a claim as established scientific fact.
treasure: Grandparents often treasure the words and phrases they heard as children, even if they are nonstandard.
She kept every letter he had ever sent her — they were a treasure she would never part with.
recognized: The word 'y'all' is now recognized as a useful second-person plural pronoun by many linguists.

low-key: The café had a low-key atmosphere, with soft music and dim lighting that encouraged long conversations.

bathroom tile: A café in Rio de Janeiro became famous for its unusual décor, including walls covered in bathroom tile.

undergoing: Rio de Janeiro was undergoing a cultural renaissance when bossa nova emerged in the late 1950s.

spreading: Bossa nova was spreading rapidly through the cafés and theatres of Rio in the early 1960s.

breakout: The singer's breakout moment came when a clip of her performance went viral on social media.

come across: I came across a fascinating article about the history of coffee while browsing online.

keep up with: It is hard to keep up with all the new vocabulary when you are learning a foreign language.

break down: The car broke down on the highway and they had to wait two hours for assistance.

throw your bags: Just throw your bags in the trunk and we can hit the road right away.

trunk: He opened the trunk of the car and loaded the heavy suitcases before the long drive.

pack a cooler: Before a long road trip, it is a good idea to pack a cooler with snacks and cold drinks.

open road: There is nothing like the feeling of the open road stretching ahead of you on a clear morning.

wind up: If you take too many wrong turns, you might wind up far from where you intended to go.

stretched: The highway stretched for hundreds of miles through flat farmland with no towns in sight.

country fairs: Country fairs were one of the most important social events for rural communities in the American South.

to sip: She liked to sip her coffee slowly in the morning before the rest of the household woke up.

messy: Drinking Turkish coffee can be messy if you are not careful, because the grounds settle at the bottom.

tough: It can be tough to break a habit you have had for years, but consistency makes a real difference.

almost powder like: Turkish coffee is made with beans ground almost powder like, far finer than any other brewing method.

tricky: Getting the water temperature right for filter coffee is tricky — too hot and it tastes bitter.
Pronouncing words in a new language can be tricky at first, but it gets easier with practice.
soothing: Many people find the ritual of making a slow cup of coffee surprisingly soothing after a long day.

church bells: Before mechanical clocks, church bells rang every hour so people could keep track of time.

for effect: He paused dramatically for effect before announcing the winner of the competition.

airplay: The new single got massive airplay on radio stations across the country within its first week.

at any given point: At any given point during rush hour, there are thousands of cars stuck on this highway.

halted: Construction on the new bridge was halted due to the unexpected heavy rain.

cross our lips: That secret must never cross our lips outside of this room — not to anyone.

folded: She folded the paper carefully and slid it inside the envelope before sealing it.

refurbish: The hotel was completely refurbished last year and now looks like a brand-new building.

factories: Many factories moved out of the city center to reduce operating costs and avoid congestion.

implies: Calling someone's feedback 'a bit harsh' implies that they may have gone too far.

sundials: Before mechanical clocks existed, sundials were used to tell the time by reading the shadow of the sun.

kept to himself: He was a quiet, reserved man who kept to himself and rarely joined conversations at the office.

no longer: I no longer drink coffee after six in the evening because it affects my sleep.

nonstandard: Using 'ain't' is considered nonstandard English and is generally avoided in formal writing.

evolved into: Country music evolved into a mainstream genre that appeals to audiences far beyond rural America.

several: She visited several countries during her three-month trip through Southeast Asia.

standard: English has become the standard language for international business and academic communication.

headquartered: The company is headquartered in New York but has regional offices in over twenty countries.

fled the drought: Thousands of farming families fled the drought and headed west in search of fertile land.
